title: What Doubts About Wealth Am I Ready to Clear?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for surfacing and examining the residual doubts about lasting prosperity
excerpt: A five-prompt journal set for Day 64 of a 12-week money program. The prompts move from the wider question of what doubt about wealth feels like to the specific belief that is still present underneath financial progress. Use it when things are going well but a background uncertainty about whether the good phase will last remains.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 64 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

content.text: A five-prompt journal set from the Day 64 theme of a 12-week money program. Week 10 focus: building unshakable trust. These prompts work with the specific doubt that persists even when financial life is improving, the background sense that the good phase is on loan, not permanent. Set a timer for ten minutes. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What does the phrase 'lasting wealth' feel like in my body right now?
Write the first thing. Don't aim for a pleasant answer.
Where am I in my relationship with financial trust right now?
Not where you want to be. Where you actually are.
What specific doubt about lasting wealth is still present for me, even when things are going well?
Name the worry as precisely as you can.
Where did that doubt come from? Whose voice is it in?
Follow it back as far as you can.
What would I need to believe about money for the doubt to be unnecessary?
Write that belief down. That's the reframe you're working toward.
Adapted from the Day 64 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
